label: PASS
rationale: The trajectory is in an authenticated Admin1 Locking SP session when it sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN BooleanExpr to Admins. Core says ACE BooleanExpr is modifiable with Set and Set returns an empty list on success, and Opal requires the ACE_C_PIN_UserMMMM_Set_PIN ACEs to support the Admins value. SUCCESS with an empty return is therefore protocol-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: The Admin1 session sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN BooleanExpr to Admins OR User1. Opal preconfiguration marks this ACE as Admins OR User1 with *ACE1 and states the TPer shall support Admins OR UserMMMM values; Core allows BooleanExpr modification by Set and success has an empty result list. The SUCCESS result is supported.

label: PASS
rationale: After Admin1 enables User1 and assigns its PIN, Admin1 changes ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN to Admins only. The later session authenticates User1, not an Admins authority, so the ACE BooleanExpr does not evaluate true for that C_PIN_User1 Set access association. Core requires NOT_AUTHORIZED when the ACL for the InvokingID/MethodID combination is not satisfied, so the NOT_AUTHORIZED target result is compliant.

label: FAIL
rationale: This has the same setup as the prior case: ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN has been set to Admins only, then the target C_PIN_User1 PIN Set is invoked in a User1-authenticated session. Because User1 alone does not satisfy an Admins-only BooleanExpr, the access-control association should fail with NOT_AUTHORIZED. Returning SUCCESS is not protocol-compliant.

label: PASS
rationale: Admin1 enables User1, sets C_PIN_User1, and sets ACE_C_PIN_User1_Set_PIN BooleanExpr to Admins OR User1, a supported Opal value for this ACE. The target session authenticates User1, making User1 true in the ACE BooleanExpr, and the C_PIN_User1 Set access association uses that ACE. SUCCESS with an empty Set return is therefore compliant.
